Protein families: Class I-like SAM-binding methyltransferase superfamily, RsmB/NOP family
Sequence similarities: SIMILARITY: Belongs to the class I-like SAM-binding methyltransferase superfamily. RsmB/NOP family. {ECO:0000255|PROSITE-ProRule:PRU01023}.
Catalytic activity: CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=cytidine(2278) in 25S rRNA + S-adenosyl-L-methionine = 5-methylcytidine(2278) in 25S rRNA + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine + H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:43180, Rhea:RHEA-COMP:10391, Rhea:RHEA-COMP:10392, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789, ChEBI:CHEBI:74483, ChEBI:CHEBI:82748; EC=2.1.1.311; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:23913415, ECO:0000269|PubMed:25125595, ECO:0000269|PubMed:25635753}; PhysiologicalDirection=left-to-right; Xref=Rhea:RHEA:43181; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:23913415, ECO:0000269|PubMed:25125595, ECO:0000269|PubMed:25635753};
Function [CC]: FUNCTION: S-adenosyl-L-methionine-dependent methyltransferase that specifically methylates the C(5) position of cytosine 2278 (m5C2278) in 25S rRNA (PubMed:23913415, PubMed:25125595, PubMed:25635753). Loss of m5C2278 in 25S rRNA results in anisomycin hypersensitivity (PubMed:23913415). {ECO:0000269|PubMed:23913415, ECO:0000269|PubMed:25125595, ECO:0000269|PubMed:25635753}.
